Should an ant-hill be found to the south of a Gray Bonduc
tree, there would be a water-vein at a depth of 17J-cubits at a
distance of 2 cubits to its south. The signs would be: a small tortoise
at a depth of 2j-cubits; there would emerge first of all an easterly
vein, then another northerly vein of sweet water; then a green stone
below; beneath that there would be water.
[It is strange that Utpala construes the word as SpT^;,]

If there be an ant-hill to the north of a Bassia tree, there
would be water at a depth of 37J cubits at a distance of five cubits
to the west of the tree. Here the indications are : first a big snake at
a depth of 5 cubits, then tawny clay, next a stone of the colour of
horse gram; next there would be an easterly vein carrying always
